channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Marcos and Trump Discuss South China Sea Concerns Ahead of Meeting with Chinese President
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. and U.S. President Donald Trump engaged in a telephone conversation to address global economic and security issues, including recent developments in the South China Sea....

item.title: Christopher Nolan's Modern Take on Homer's Odyssey
item.description: Christopher Nolan's adaptation of Homer's *The Odyssey* is a tactile and immersive retelling aimed at modern audiences, though critics argue it sanitizes the original epic. The article explores Nolan'...

item.title: Madlanga Commission Restricts Discussion of Suliman Carrim's Health During Testimony
item.description: The Madlanga Commission has imposed strict restrictions on discussing Suliman Carrim's health during his upcoming testimony. Chair Justice Mbuyiseli Madlanga ordered that no mention of Carrim's medica...

item.title: Elon Musk Faces Fact-Check Over Inflammatory X Posts
item.description: Elon Musk, the CEO of Tesla and SpaceX, faced intense scrutiny after an interview with The Economist highlighted his controversial statements about the United Kingdom. During the discussion, Musk clai...

item.title: Tanzania Focuses on Peace, Tourism, and Sustainable Development
item.description: The article highlights key developments in Tanzania on July 23, 2026. The African Alliance for Freedom and Peace (AAFP) calls for unity and peace to drive national progress, emphasizing the need for c...

item.title: US Imposes Tariffs on 60 Partners, Including Pakistan, Over Forced Labour Concerns
item.description: The United States has imposed new tariffs on 60 trading partners, including Pakistan, India, China, and the European Union, over concerns regarding forced labor practices. The tariffs, effective from ...

item.title: Dual citizen pleads guilty to smuggling operation resulting in fatal river accident
item.description: Stephanie Square, a dual Canadian-American citizen, has pleaded guilty to her role in a human smuggling operation that led to the deaths of a Romanian family of four and a boat operator in the St. Law...

item.title: BAE Systems CEO warns of UK defense complacency amid Russian threat
item.description: Dr Charles Woodburn, CEO of BAE Systems, has warned that the UK has been overly complacent about the risk of foreign attack, citing the highest threat level he has ever observed. He emphasized the nee...

item.title: Analysts Note Prolonged Standing Ovation for Trump at EPA Speech
item.description: Political analysts expressed bewilderment after President Donald Trump received an unusually long standing ovation following his speech unveiling the 'Ratepayer Protection Pledge' at the Environmental...

item.title: UK PM's High-Stakes Nuclear Power Deal
item.description: The article discusses the significant political and economic challenges faced by the newly appointed UK Prime Minister as they navigate a complex nuclear energy agreement with international stakeholde...

item.title: Pakistan reaffirms stance against Houthi attacks on maritime assets
item.description: Pakistan has issued a firm warning to Yemen's Houthi rebels, stating that any attack on its maritime interests will be treated as an act of aggression against the country. Foreign Office spokesperson ...

item.title: Sophie Thatcher Reflects on Leaving 'Yellowjackets' and Her Future Roles
item.description: Sophie Thatcher, 25, has confirmed she is leaving the Paramount+ series 'Yellowjackets' after its fourth and final season. In an interview with Variety, she expressed that portraying teenage Natalie S...

item.title: BOSA terminates Ayanda Allie's membership after disciplinary process
item.description: The Board of Build One South Africa (BOSA) has officially terminated the party membership of Ayanda Allie, a member of the Gauteng Provincial Legislature. This decision followed a prolonged disciplina...

item.title: Arrest of Mamasapano Massacre Suspect
item.description: A former member of the Bangsamoro Islamic Freedom Fighters-Dawlah Islamiya (BIFF-DI), implicated in the 2015 massacre of 44 Philippine National Police Special Action Force (SAF) commandos, was arreste...

item.title: Tragic mining accident in Queensland leaves one worker dead and another critically injured
item.description: A fatal workplace incident occurred at the BMA-owned Peak Downs coal mine near Moranbah, central Queensland. A light vehicle was struck by a large mining truck around 4:30am on Friday, resulting in th...

item.title: Delhi University Advises Students to Avoid Protests at Jantar Mantar Amid Legal Concerns
item.description: The University of Delhi has issued a social media appeal urging students and faculty to avoid participating in the ongoing CJP-led protest at Jantar Mantar, citing safety risks and potential legal con...

item.title: Contrasting Approaches to Tobacco and Opioid Regulation: A Call for Coherence in Harm Reduction Strategies
item.description: The article by Chris Selley critiques the inconsistency in public health policies regarding addiction. While tobacco stigmatization and restrictive measures have successfully reduced smoking rates ove...

item.title: NDC Candidate Calls for Transparent Tax Enforcement in Kwara
item.description: Prof. Abdulmumin Yinka Ajia, NDC's gubernatorial candidate in Kwara State, has criticized the Kwara State Internal Revenue Service (KW IRS) for using heavy-handed tax enforcement measures. He demanded...

item.title: PIC governance crisis escalates as Masondo resigns amid board resignations and BEE financing probe
item.description: Deputy Finance Minister David Masondo has resigned as chairperson of the Public Investment Corporation (PIC) board, citing the need to safeguard the stability of the state asset manager and maintain p...

item.title: Supreme Court allows industries in Taj zone with environmental safeguards
item.description: The Supreme Court (SC) has lifted a two-year-old blanket ban on establishing industries in the Taj Trapezium Zone (TTZ), a sensitive area near the Taj Mahal. This decision opens avenues for employment...
